torch_pattern: cos = torch.nn.functional.embedding(rot_idxs, cos_matrix); sin = torch.nn.functional.embedding(rot_idxs, sin_matrix)  # two sibling lookups sharing one index tensor (golden: torch.nn.functional.embedding(input_tensor, weight) per branch)

config_template.num_branches: {N} (2 for cos/sin RoPE; 3 for word/token_type/position BERT embeddings each with own index tensor)

weight_transform: each branch keeps its own table (cos_matrix/sin_matrix or per-embedding weight) as ttnn.from_torch(table, ROW_MAJOR_LAYOUT); no transform. Branch outputs typically combined downstream via add (BERT) or consumed in parallel (RoPE).
applicability_notes: N sibling ttnn.embedding lookups sharing one index tensor (rot_idxs) — candidates for merged dispatch. Outputs then unsqueeze_to_4D + transpose(1,2) for RoPE decode.; ttnn_replacement: cos = ttnn.embedding(rot_idxs, cos_matrix, layout={LAYOUT}, memory_config={MEMORY_CONFIG}); sin = ttnn.embedding(rot_idxs, sin_matrix, layout={LAYOUT}, memory_config={MEMORY_CONFIG})

placement_observations.source: call_site: cos/sin = ttnn.embedding(rot_idxs, {cos,sin}_matrix, layout=TILE_LAYOUT) with default/DRAM memory_config in standard decode path

call_site: embedding_layout = ttnn.ROW_MAJOR_LAYOUT; cos/sin = ttnn.embedding(rot_idxs, matrix, layout=embedding_layout, memory_config=mem_config) with sharded mem_config built via use_height_and_width_as_shard_shape=True
